title: Jasminum collinum
taxonomy: Jasminum collinum belongs to the family Oleaceae, a group of flowering plants commonly known as the olive family. This family includes various species of trees and shrubs that are native to temperate and subtropical regions. Jasminum collinum is a species of jasmine, a genus of flowering plants that are known for their fragrant flowers and are native to tropical and subtropical regions. The species is native to Sri Lanka and is often found in the country's tropical forests. It is a climbing shrub that uses its twining stems to support its growth.
uses: Jasminum collinum, commonly found in tropical regions, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can help in treating skin infections and wounds. In Ayurvedic medicine, it is used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The leaves and flowers of the plant are also believed to have antioxidant properties, which can help in protecting against cell damage. Additionally, it is used to treat digestive issues and is also believed to have anti-cancer properties.
benefits: Jasminum collinum, also known as the Star Jasmine, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. constituents: Jasminum collinum is known to contain flavonoids, which are a class of plant compounds that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. parts: Jasminum collinum is a plant species known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Indian medicine. preparations: Jasminum collinum, also known as the climbing jasmine, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. intro: Jasminum collinum, also known as Indian jasmine or Star jasmine, is a flowering vine native to the Western Ghats in India. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including stress, anxiety, and insomnia, due to its adaptogenic properties and ability to promote relaxation. The bioactive constituents of Jasminum collinum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional Ayurvedic medicine, Jasminum collinum is often prepared into herbal teas, capsules, or extracts to promote relaxation, improve sleep quality, and reduce stress levels.
